products.title: UNS Reef System - R240
products.body_html:
The UNS R240 Reef System is the largest aquarium in the UNS lineup. This model sets itself apart from the standard reef series with a euro-braced top for enhanced structural security, dual overflows, and a reinforced stand. It retains UNS’s signature craftsmanship, including 45° mitered edges, black silicone, and a double-pane glass bottom. The aluminum-framed stand features double support bars on the back and additional horizontal reinforcement for superior stability. Its magnetic, fully removable doors provide effortless access for routine maintenance. The R240 also includes an expanded electrical box with increased space for dosing containers and controllers.

Important Shipping & Handling Notice: The UNS R240 Reef System requires freight service and specialized handling. Orders will have extended processing and transit times compared to standard items and regular freight serviced orders. Delivery timelines are estimates only and may change based on service provider, logistics, and final destination.

Rimless Aquarium Features

  • Signature 45° mitered edges and low-iron, high-clarity glass provide an enjoyable viewing experience and enable closer edge-to-edge cleaning with the preferred glass cleaning tool/algae scraper.
  • Black silicone discreetly hides the appearance of coralline
  • A fully Euro-braced top
  • Tanks feature a sleek white bottom, allowing for a bare-bottom setup without exposing equipment in the sump.

Tank Specifications

  • Dimensions: 94.49 x 31.50 x 23.62" / 240 x 80 x 60CM
  • Glass Thickness: 19mm 
  • Volume: 265.34G / 1004.421L
  • Total Volume (Including Sump): 327.34 Gallons

Stand Specifications

  • Dimensions: 94.49 x 31.50 x 36.26" / 240 x 80 x 92.1CM
  • Door Count: 6
  • Storage Drawers: 1

Sump Specifications

  • Dimensions: 66.85 x 28.66 x 17.72" / 169.8 x 72.8 x 45CM
  • Volume: 62G / 235L 
  • Filter Socks: 8
  • Total Chambers: 4 + ATO

What's Included

  • UNS R240 Rimless Aquarium
  • Pipe Fittings
  • Union Fittings
  • Outlet Nozzles
  • UNS R240 Stand & Sump

Aquarium Cabinet & Sump Features

  • The two overflow boxes feature a low-profile, intake slots at the top and bottom and a vertical piece of glass inside that provides safeguard against flooding of the main tank in the event of pump failure.
  • The overflow lid cover is made from polycarbonate material for increased durability and resistance to heat and warping. Other covers are designed for easy removal and sit flush to reduce salt creep.
  • Fully removable, magnetic doors. Aluminum frame and magnetic hold remove the need for traditional stainless steel door hinges, improving rust resistance.
  • Comes fully assembled with sump pre-installed for easy setup and straightforward plumbing. Simply place the tank on top, connect the pre-glued pipes and secure each connection with easy twist-on unions.
  • Pre-cut holes for wire management.
  • A pull-out drawer for extra storage and organization of accessories, tools and more.
  • Built-in controller box provides a dedicated space for a surge protector and optimal cable management. 
  • Extra controller box storage for dosing containers
  • Controller boards are removable and can be easily accessed by removing the side door while ensuring wires remain tucked away.

Sump Filtration System Features

  • Gate valves are included for smooth water flow and reduced noise. Additional ball valve and barbed fittings are included on the return line in the event external equipment, such as reactors or chillers are being used. Included a gasket below the drain pipe and sump to reduce salt creep accumulation.
  • The multi-chamber sump features a prefilter baffle to trap larger solids on filter floss, reducing the frequency of filter sock replacement. Diffuser plates are included to reduce noise and prevent floating of filter socks when replacement/maintenance is near.
  • The second chamber will accommodate a variety of protein skimmers on the market. Water levels can be adjusted via the acrylic baffle to accommodate various height requirements of different protein skimmers.
  • The third chamber may be used for additional desired media or as a refugium. Water levels may be adjusted as desired.
  • Chamber on the far left is a dedicated ATO reservoir (16G ATO) for use with an optical ATO

Available in 7 tank sizes and two stand colors

    • UNS R60
    • UNS R80
    • UNS R90
    • UNS R120
    • UNS R150
    • UNS R180
    • UNS R240

products.title: UNS 75A - 40.1 Gallon Dual AIO Rimless Aquarium
products.body_html:
The UNS 75A is a 40.1-gallon Dual All-In-One Tank that includes a native, fully-integrated internal filtration system that eliminates the need for another piece of equipment. The built-in filter features a quadruple-chamber that effectively screens out debris and includes a coarse aquarium sponge, quality biological media, and two quiet but powerful water pumps that ensure healthy circulation throughout the entire system. The Dual AIO series can be customized with optional accessories to support a successful freshwater or saltwater aquarium setup.

The Ultum Nature Systems line of Dual All-In-One rimless aquariums builds upon the cherished features of the standard rimless aquarium, which include flawlessly crafted 45° precision cut mitered edges, unobtrusive silicone, and unparalleled clarity thanks to the low-iron, 91% Diamant glass formula.

Key Features and Benefits

The Ultum Nature Systems line of Dual All-In-One Tanks comes with everything necessary for a solid foundation.

  • UNS Rimless Tank: Crafted with precision and elegance, this rimless tank houses your ecosystem. Constructed of low-iron, 91% Diamant glass formula for unparalleled clarity.
  • Leveling Mat: Specifically designed to support and stabilize the aquarium's weight while protecting against glass fissures and cracks, ensuring overall structural integrity and resilience.
  • Quadruple Chamber Internal Filter: A native, fully-integrated internal filtration system that eliminates the need for more equipment, which can detract from the beauty of the aquascape. Features internal filtration chambers that effectively screen out debris.
  • Coarse Aquarium Sponge: Serves as mechanical filtration and occupies the first filtration chamber. Prevents fish waste and plant matter from clogging up the other chambers responsible for screening out finer debris particles.
  • UNS Bio Block: Serves as biological filtration and provides increased surface area for beneficial bacteria to colonize, resulting in increased biological filtration capacity.

Product Specifications

  • Dimensions (LxWxH): 29.52" x 17.71" x 17.71"
  • Tank Volume: 40.1 Gallons

Accessory Compatibility Guide & Configuration Options

Chambers 1 & 4

  • Use the included black filter sponge, or
  • Upgrade with the filter bag conversion kit, compatible with:
    • Honeycomb filter socks
    • Nylon mesh filter socks
  • Optional intake guards can be installed to reduce intake opening size and help protect livestock from being drawn into the rear chamber.
  • Dimensions (LxWxH): 3.11 x 3.94 x 17.36" // 7.9 x 10 x 44.09CM

Chamber 2

  • Features a fixed water height, making it ideal for:
    • UNS Refugium
    • UNS Dual All-in-One Protein Skimmer
    • Dimensions (LxWxH): 7.48 x 3.94 x 17.36" // 19 x 10 x 44.09CM

Chamber 3

  • Supports dual pumps, with each pump connected to its own outflow nozzle for increased flow. Aquarium includes two pumps, with an optional Y-splitter for users who prefer a single-pump setup.
  • Dimensions (LxWxH): 14 x 3.94 x 17.36" // 35.56 x 10 x 44.09CM

What’s Included

  • 1x UNS Rimless Tank
  • 1x Leveling Mat
  • 2x Media Riser Trays
  • 2x Filter Sponges
  • 8x UNS Bio Blocks (Filter Media Block) 
  • 2x Submersible Water Pump
  • Y Splitter
  • 2x Outflow Nozzles
  • 2x Back Chamber Lids

Looking to upgrade and customize your UNS Dual AIO system? Click here to browse compatible add-on accessories.

products.title: Bucephalandra sp. Neo
products.body_html:
Common Name: Bucephalandra Neo

Bucephalandra ‘Neo’ is a compact aquatic plant with clean form and subtle iridescence. Its narrow, wavy leaves grow in tight clusters, ranging from deep green to blue-green with undertones of red. As an epiphyte, Buce Neo is easy to care for and thrives in low to medium lighting without CO2 when attached to rock or driftwood.

Under stronger lighting, it can develop a subtle metallic sheen that adds depth and dimension to its already rich coloration. It stays low and tidy as it grows, making it an excellent choice for adding finer detail and structure to the foreground without overwhelming the layout.

Notes:

  • Bucephalandra is an epiphytic aquarium plant, and prefers to be attached to aquarium hardscape.
  • Do not cover the rhizome when planting the Bucephalandra in substrate. This will cause melt and rot.
  • Please be sure to remove this plant from its pot. Remove the rockwool surrounding the roots and attach it to the hardscape. For instructions on how to properly prep "potted" aquarium plants, click here.
  • Bucephalandra’s final size, coloration, and growth rate are dependent upon the condition of the aquarium.
  • Photos are a representation of what you will receive and may vary.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters can result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • CO2 injection will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Family Name: Araceae
Endemic To: Borneo
Leaves: Small
pH: 6-8
Care: Easy
Light: Low to Medium
CO2: Not necessary, but recommended
Propagation: Cut by rhizome
Growth rate: Slow

products.title: Hydrocotyle Tripartita Mini Variegated UNS Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
Common Name: Hydrocotyle Japan Mini

Hydrocotyle Tripartita Mini is a smaller variation of the regular Hydrocotyle Tripartita, sometimes referred to as Hydrocotyle Japan. This aquatic plant is a great choice for midground placement and is characterized by its clover-shaped leaves and bright green color. When grown in optimal conditions, Hydrocotyle Tripartita Mini grows into compact bushes that are commonly seen in planted aquariums. Like the original, this plant is versatile and depending on tank size, can be used in the foreground, midground or background. It is undemanding and grows fast, often out-competing and overgrowing the tank if left untrimmed. By frequently trimming new growth and using high light with CO2, the plant can stay low growing and create a carpeting effect over the foreground.

Propagate by trimming stems and replanting on pulling apart dense clumps and splitting the roots. New shoots can be trimmed anywhere along the stem which allows the plant to grow thicker and more compact. 

Key Features & Important Notes:

  • Tissue culture plants are grown in-vitro from state of the art laboratories that specialize in propagating aquatic plants. They provide the best option for those who want to ensure that absolutely zero unwanted pests are introduced into the aquarium.
  • Remove tissue culture gel before planting. Simply wash the gel away or soak the plant in water and squeeze the gel off gently with your fingers.
  • Cryptocoryne prefer not to be moved once planted. If possible, do not move them.
  • For instructions on how to properly plant tissue culture aquarium plants, click here.
  • Refrain from making drastic or sudden changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters contribute to plant melt.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Species Information:

Origin: New Zealand, Australia
Height: 5-10"
pH: 6.5-7.5
Care: Easy to Moderate
Light: Moderate
Co2: Recommended
Propagation:Cut stem and replant
Growth rate: Fast

products.title: 2HR Aquarist - APT Fix
products.body_html:
APT Fix by 2HR Aquarist is a safe, fast-acting solution for controlling common aquarium algae without harsh chemicals. Designed to tackle stubborn algae like Black Beard Algae (BBA), hair algae, string algae, fuzz, and other filamentous varieties, it allows precise spot treatment to target problem areas without affecting the entire tank.

This copper-free, fish-safe, and shrimp-safe aquarium algae treatment biodegrades after use and is ideal for planted aquariums with hardy, established plants and sensitive livestock.

Size: 300ml 
Directions: Each bottle comes with a convenient spot-dosing pipette, so you can apply up to 1 ml per 10 L directly to affected areas.

Key Features and Important Notes

  • Fast-acting algae control - visibly reduces algae growth quickly without harming your aquascape
  • Targets multiple types of algae - effective against BBA, hair, string, and filamentous algae
  • Safe for fish and shrimp - copper-free formula ensures aquatic life remains unharmed

Guaranteed Analysis

  • 3% Epoxy Aldehydes

products.title: 2HR Aquarist - APT 1 Zero
products.body_html:
APT 1 Zero by 2HR Aquarist is a concentrated daily fertilizer made for planted aquariums. The formula supports strong growth and vibrant color without extra nitrates or phosphates, making it ideal for new tanks or heavily stocked aquariums where fish waste already supplies a steady source of nutrients. In a fish-heavy setup, APT 1 Zero provides essential macronutrients for balanced plant nutrition while helping to minimize algae issues. The included pump is calibrated for easy and convenient dosing. Ensure your aquarium plants get what they need to thrive while keeping your aquascape clean and healthy.

APT 1 Zero is the simple, effective solution for aquascapers who want strong, vibrant aquatic plants without adding extra nitrates or phosphates.

Size: 300ml 
Directions: Each bottle includes a calibrated pump. (1ml per press)
Dose 3ml per 26 gallons (100L) daily.

Key Features and Important Notes

  • Concentrated, balanced daily formula for strong, healthy aquarium plant growth
  • 2x higher potassium for improved colors
  • Nitrate and phosphate-free
  • Ideal for new aquariums and fish-heavy planted tanks
  • One 300ml bottle lasts ~3 months / 30 gallon tank (YMMV)
  • Includes calibrated pump for convenient, precise and easy dosing

Guaranteed Analysis

  • Soluble Potash (K₂O): 5.5%
  • Magnesium (Mg): 0.85%
  • Iron (Fe): 0.1% (0.1% chelated)

Derived from: Potassium Sulfate, Potassium Chloride, Magnesium Sulfate, DTPA Iron, EDTA Iron, EDDHA Iron

products.title: 2HR Aquarist - APT 3 Complete
products.body_html:
APT3 by 2HR Aquarist is a comprehensive liquid fertilizer specially formulated for densely planted freshwater aquariums. Its calibrated Capstone Formula supplies complete nutrition—macro, micro, and trace elements, that support strong growth and vivid color for even the most high-demand aquatic plant species. This well-rounded nutrient solution is engineered to support demanding species while keeping nutrition balanced. The formula includes nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, magnesium, iron, and a full suite of essential trace elements—all fully water‑soluble for reliable uptake.

With daily dosing, plants can access the nutrients they need for efficient photosynthesis and healthy leaf development. Its nutrient ratios are designed to encourage deeper coloration and lush growth without unnecessary spikes in any single element. So, whether you're running a high-tech CO2 setup or a densely planted low-tech tank, APT 3 delivers reliable everyday nutrition to support robust growth in a planted aquarium.

Size: 300ml
Directions: Each bottle includes a calibrated pump. (1ml per press)
Dose 3ml per 26 gallons (100L) daily.

Key Features and Important Notes

  • Complete nutrient blend for planted tanks
  • Ideal for mature tanks, densely planted setups
  • One 300ml bottle lasts ~3 months / 30 gallon tank (YMMV)
  • Includes calibrated pump for convenient, precise and easy dosing

Guaranteed Analysis

  • Total Nitrogen (N): 0.75% (0.75% Nitrate Nitrogen)
  • Avalable Phosphoric Acid (P2O5): 0.69%
  • Soluble Potash (K₂O): 4.52%
  • Magnesium (Mg): 1.10%
  • Iron (Fe): 0.12% (0.12% chelated)

Derived from: Potassium Sulphate, Potassium Phosphate, Potassium Chloride, Magnesium Nitrate, Magnesium Sulphate, DTPA Iron, EDTA Iron, EDDHA Iron

products.title: Dragon Wood Showpiece (DW13)
products.body_html:
Make choosing the right piece easy with our WYSIWYG (What You See Is What You Get) Driftwood Showpieces. These one-of-a-kind pieces are hand-selected for their unique shape and size and photographed so that what you see is the piece that arrives at your door. Perfect for aquariums, terrariums, paludariums, or reptile enclosures, these natural pieces simplify hardscape planning and bring your vision to life without any surprises.

Dragon Wood stands out for its dark brown tones and rugged texture. It has deep, natural crevices that offer a dramatic "old-growth" aesthetic, reminiscent of decaying timber found along forest riverbeds. This Dragon Wood showpiece will provide a great surface for anchoring aquatic mosses, Anubias, or Ferns, perfect for hobbyists looking to create a woodland-themed hardscape.

Approximate Size: 67.5L x 33.5W x 27.5H inches

The Pen shown is provided for size reference.

Browse our entire collection of WYSIWYG Showpieces

Notes:

  • Tannins: All natural driftwood can leach tannins, which may create a slight yellow or brown "tea-stained" tint in your aquarium water. This is a natural process and will gradually diminish over time through regular water changes.
  • Buoyancy: Driftwood is naturally buoyant and will float initially. To ensure your showpiece stays in place, you can submerge the wood in a separate container until it is fully waterlogged. Alternatively, boiling the wood for 1–2 hours not only speeds up the process but also helps release tannins and sterilizes the piece.
  • Immediate Placement: You can secure the wood using aquarium-safe glue or weigh it down with a large stone or heavy décor until it remains submerged naturally.
  • Biofilm: Shortly after submersion, a white biofilm may develop on the wood; this is a natural bacterial growth that will disappear on its own or be eaten by shrimp and algae-eaters.
  • Important Note: We always recommend rinsing your wood thoroughly with fresh water before adding it to an established aquarium, terrarium, or enclosure to remove any surface dust or debris.

products.title: Dragon Wood Showpiece (DW11)
products.body_html:
Make choosing the right piece easy with our WYSIWYG (What You See Is What You Get) Driftwood Showpieces. These one-of-a-kind pieces are hand-selected for their unique shape and size and photographed so that what you see is the piece that arrives at your door. Perfect for aquariums, terrariums, paludariums, or reptile enclosures, these natural pieces simplify hardscape planning and bring your vision to life without any surprises.

Dragon Wood stands out for its dark brown tones and rugged texture. It has deep, natural crevices that offer a dramatic "old-growth" aesthetic, reminiscent of decaying timber found along forest riverbeds. This Dragon Wood showpiece will provide a great surface for anchoring aquatic mosses, Anubias, or Ferns, perfect for hobbyists looking to create a woodland-themed hardscape.

Approximate Size: 82L x 20W x 25H inches

The Pen shown is provided for size reference.

Browse our entire collection of WYSIWYG Showpieces

Notes:

  • Tannins: All natural driftwood can leach tannins, which may create a slight yellow or brown "tea-stained" tint in your aquarium water. This is a natural process and will gradually diminish over time through regular water changes.
  • Buoyancy: Driftwood is naturally buoyant and will float initially. To ensure your showpiece stays in place, you can submerge the wood in a separate container until it is fully waterlogged. Alternatively, boiling the wood for 1–2 hours not only speeds up the process but also helps release tannins and sterilizes the piece.
  • Immediate Placement: You can secure the wood using aquarium-safe glue or weigh it down with a large stone or heavy décor until it remains submerged naturally.
  • Biofilm: Shortly after submersion, a white biofilm may develop on the wood; this is a natural bacterial growth that will disappear on its own or be eaten by shrimp and algae-eaters.
  • Important Note: We always recommend rinsing your wood thoroughly with fresh water before adding it to an established aquarium, terrarium, or enclosure to remove any surface dust or debris.

products.title: Boutique Seiryu Stone Showpiece (BSSP2010)
products.body_html:
What you see is what you get!

Shown in UNS 25C Tank (9.84" x 9.84" x 9.84")

Boutique Seiryu Stone Showpieces are carefully crafted using select Boutique Seiryu Stones, hand picked for their unique shapes and details. Each piece has been cut and flattened on one end to create a flat surface which allows you to easily place it into your aquarium. Instantly create an intricate mountain scape with minimal effort by simply placing the Boutique Seiryu Stone Showpiece into your tank, adding your substrate and planting throughout.  

NOTE: Photos show the single listed showpiece from different angles. A single piece works perfectly in a cube shaped tank as the center focal point, or use multiple pieces placed side by side in a longer aquarium.

products.title: UNS Controlock
products.body_html:
The ideal solution for reinforcing aquarium hardscape structures with strong, lasting bonds. Apply it between contact points and secure with your favorite liquid cyanoacrylate glue for a permanent hold. A brief smoking reaction is normal as it sets—allow one minute to dry for a firm, reliable bond. Safe for aquarium fish, shrimp, invertebrates, and plants.

Each order of Controlock includes one container of bonding material and one bottle of UNS Controbond (liquid cyanoacrylate adhesive). When paired, the combo creates the perfect solution for reinforcing aquarium hardscape structures. 

Size: 60g / 20g adhesive
Directions: Pinch and pack between hardscape. Add liquid adhesive and let it cure fully before moving or filling the aquarium.

Key Features and Important Notes

  • Create unique aquascape layouts with confidence! 
  • Use this product in well-ventilated areas.
  • Avoid contact with eyes and skin.
  • Keep out of reach of children and pets.

What's Included

  • 1 UNS Controlock Bonding Material
  • 1 UNS Controbond Liquid Adhesive
  • 1 Pair of UNS Controlock Tweezers
  • 1 UNS Controbond Anti-Dry Cap
  • 2 UNS Controbond Precision Tips

How to Use Controlock to bond hardscape material!

products.title: Murdannia Keisak UNS Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
Common Name: Marsh Dewflower, Asian Spiderwort

Murdannia Keisak is an uncommon stem plant that adds a wild, grassy look to planted aquariums. This bamboo-like aquatic marsh plant features thin, upright stems with narrow green leaves that spread in a natural, untamed pattern—unlike the more structured growth of Rotalas or Ludwigias. It’s slightly messy, organic look makes it an ideal choice for nature-style aquariums, biotopes, or paludariums, where that “intentional wildness” brings a more realistic feel to the aquascape.

While Asian Spiderwort is considered a semi-aquatic plant typically found in its emersed form in nature, it can be grown in both submerged and emersed conditions under high lighting and in nutrient-rich water. Providing this plant with CO2 is optional, but will promote faster, fuller growth. With minimal demands and versatile growth, it’s a rewarding plant for aquascapers seeking a natural accent.

Notes:

  • Murdannia Keisak can be grown in both submerged and emersed environments
  • Tissue culture plants are grown in vitro in a state of the art laboratory specialized in propagating aquatic plants. They provide the best option for those who want to ensure that absolutely zero unwanted pests, pesticides, or algae are introduced into the aquarium.
  • Remove tissue culture gel before planting. Simply wash the gel away or soak the plant in water and squeeze the gel off with your fingers.
  • For instructions on how to properly plant tissue culture aquarium plants, click here.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.

Family Name: Commelinaceae
Origin: Asia
Height: 2-8+"
pH: 5.0 - 7.5
Care: Medium
Light: Medium to High
CO2: Not necessary, but recommended
Propagation: Cut stem and replant
Growth rate: Fast

products.title: Hakkai Stone
products.body_html:
Hakkai Stone is a premium natural rock prized for its dark bluish-green color and smooth, river-worn texture. Also known as Pahai Stone, this stone is adorned with irregular holes and craters produced over centuries by natural erosion, giving it a unique weathered character and the nickname "Bug-Eaten Rock." Its dark coloration provides a visually striking contrast against vibrant green plants, making it a perfect pick for Iwagumi layouts or authentic riverbed-style hardscape designs. While a rare commodity in the planted aquarium hobby, Hakkai Stone is also suitable for non-aquarium use and will work beautifully as a high-end accent in terrariums, paludariums, or similar planted enclosures to achieve a mature, aged look.

Notes: 

  • Hakkai stone is very heavy. If you are looking for multiple pieces, please increase the amount ordered. If you have any questions, please contact our support team before placing your order.
  • This stone is inert and will not alter water parameters.
  • This is a natural product and has the potential to alter water parameters slightly.
  • We recommend rinsing stones thoroughly before use to remove any natural dust or sediment.
  • Stone coloration darkens when wet.

featured_image.alt: Hakkai Stone showing dark bluish-green color and smooth river-worn texture
Assorted sizes of rare Hakkai Stone
Hakkai Stone accent pieces

products.title: UNS Controbond
products.body_html:
UNS Controbond is an aquarium-safe liquid adhesive designed to securely bond hardscape materials during aquascaping. Ideal for fixing stones, driftwood, and layout structures in place, it helps create stable, long-lasting scapes without harming fish, shrimp, or plants once cured. Easy to apply and reliable, Controbond allows aquascapers to build with confidence and precision.

When paired with UNS Controlock, the combo creates the perfect solution for reinforcing aquarium hardscape structures. 

Size: 20g
Directions: Pinch and pack Controlock between hardscape, then add Controbond and allow it to cure fully.

Key Features and Important Notes

  • Create unique aquascape layouts with confidence! 
  • Liquid cyanoacrylate adhesive.
  • Aquarium-safe for fish, shrimp and aquarium plants when used as directed.
  • Use this product in well-ventilated areas and avoid contact with eyes and skin.
  • Keep out of reach of children and pets.

When paired with UNS Controlock, Controbond securely combines hardscape material and creates an ultra-strong hold for your aquascape. See it in action below!

Ghost Wood being secured to Gobi Desert Stone using Controbond and Controlock in Light Brown

Stacked Boutique Seiryu Stone being bonded with Controlock in Grey for extra security and reinforced structural integrity

products.title: Hottonia Palustris UNS Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
Common Name: Hottonia Palustris, Featherfoil

Hottonia Palustris, native to Asia and Europe, is a delicate-looking aquatic stem plant with soft, feathery, bright green leaves that form fan-shaped patterns along an upright stem, giving it a fluffy look underwater. When grown emersed, it can produce small lavender flowers that float above the surface.

In the aquarium, this stem plant works beautifully in the midground to background. With good light and nutrients, it grows quickly and develops thicker, bushier tops, perfect for creating soft texture and contrast against broad-leaved plants. In lower light, it stretches taller and stays a bit more sparse. Regular trimming keeps it compact and encourages side shoots, helping you build that full, layered look instead of a single tall stem.

Notes:

  • Tissue culture plants are grown in vitro in a state of the art laboratory specialized in propagating aquatic plants. They provide the best option for those who want to ensure that absolutely zero unwanted pests, pesticides, or algae are introduced into the aquarium.
  • Remove tissue culture gel before planting. Simply wash the gel away or soak the plant in water and squeeze the gel off with your fingers.  
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • For instructions on how to properly plant tissue culture aquarium plants, click here.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.

Family Name: Primulaceae
pH: 6-7.5
Care: Easy
Light: Low to Medium
Co2: Not necessary, but recommended
Propagation: Cut by rhizome
Growth rate: Slow

products.title: Staurogyne Repens Variegated UNS Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
Common Name: S. Repens

Staurogyne Repens Variegated is a rare twist on the classic foreground favorite. It keeps the same compact, bushy shape as standard S. Repens, but its leaves are splashed with creamy white or pale yellow streaks that really pop against darker greens. This low-growing aquatic plant originates from South America and features small, spade-shaped leaves perfect for filling in the foreground or midground of an aquascape

Like most variegated plants, it thrives under stable lighting, CO2, and nutrient levels to keep its lighter patches vibrant rather than faded. Vertical, thin growth indicates insufficient light, yellowing leaves suggest a nutrient deficiency, and thinning leaves point to low CO2. When grown under optimal conditions, this plant can grow quickly and will need regular trimming. These trimmings can be replanted to propagate new plants.

Notes:

  • Staurogyne Repens is a great coverage plant, commonly used as a foreground or midground plant
  • Staurogyne Repens can be grown in both submerged and emersed environments
  • Tissue culture plants are grown in vitro in a state of the art laboratory specialized in propagating aquatic plants. They provide the best option for those who want to ensure that absolutely zero unwanted pests, pesticides, or algae are introduced into the aquarium.
  • Remove tissue culture gel before planting. Simply wash the gel away or soak the plant in water and squeeze the gel off with your fingers.  
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • For instructions on how to properly plant tissue culture aquarium plants, click here.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.

Family Name: Acanthaceae
Origin: South America
Height: 1-3”
pH: 6-7.5
Care: Medium to Difficult
Light: Medium to High
Co2: Required
Propagation: Cut stem and replant
Growth rate: Moderate to Fast

products.title: UNS Reef System - R80CS
products.body_html:
The UNS Reef System delivers a refined, reef-ready solution available in a range of thoughtfully designed sizes. The R line of rimless aquariums showcases UNS’s signature 45° mitered edges, black silicone seams, and thicker ultra-clear glass, engineered for long-term durability and exceptional clarity. Each system is paired with a matching aluminum-framed stand and an integrated sump filtration system, creating a cohesive and professional setup from top to bottom. Magnetic, fully removable doors allow for effortless access, streamlined maintenance, and personalized equipment layouts. Larger models also include built-in storage drawers to keep essential aquarium supplies organized and within reach. Every detail of the UNS Reef System has been carefully considered to strike the ideal balance between functionality, performance, and modern aesthetics. Learn more about the features below.

The R80CS features the same footprint as the R80 Frag holding tank, while incorporating the back chamber of our standard reef systems.

Rimless Aquarium Features

  • Signature 45° mitered edges combined with low-iron, high-clarity glass provide an immersive viewing experience and allow for close, edge-to-edge cleaning with preferred glass cleaning tools or algae scrapers.
  • Black silicone subtly conceals coralline algae buildup for a cleaner, more refined appearance.
  • Thicker glass construction eliminates the need for euro-bracing while maintaining structural integrity.
  • A sleek white bottom panel supports bare-bottom reef setups while keeping sump equipment visually concealed.

Aquarium Cabinet & Sump Features

  • The R80 and R80CS include an integrated electrical cabinet and pull-out storage drawer located on the right side of the stand.
  • The low-profile overflow box features intake slots at both the top and bottom, along with an internal vertical glass baffle that helps protect against main tank flooding in the event of pump failure.
  • The overflow lid is constructed from durable polycarbonate for improved heat resistance and reduced warping, while additional covers are flush-fitting and easily removable to minimize salt creep.
  • Fully removable magnetic doors eliminate the need for traditional stainless steel hinges, increasing corrosion resistance and simplifying access.
  • The stand arrives fully assembled with the sump pre-installed, allowing for quick setup and straightforward plumbing—simply place the tank, connect the pre-glued pipes, and secure each union with easy twist-on fittings.
  • Pre-cut wire management ports help keep cables organized and out of sight.
    A built-in controller box provides a dedicated space for surge protectors and clean cable routing.
  • Removable controller boards allow easy access from the side door while keeping wiring neatly managed.

Sump Filtration System Features

  • Included gate valves ensure precise water flow control and quieter operation. A supplemental ball valve and barbed fittings are included on the return line for optional external equipment such as reactors or chillers. A gasket beneath the drainpipe and sump helps reduce salt creep buildup.
  • The multi-chamber sump incorporates a pre-filter baffle designed to capture larger debris using filter floss, extending the time between filter sock changes. Diffuser plates help reduce noise and prevent filter socks from floating during maintenance.
    The second chamber accommodates a wide range of protein skimmers, with adjustable water levels via an acrylic baffle to support varying skimmer height requirements.
  • The third chamber can be configured for additional filtration media or used as a refugium, with adjustable water levels for added flexibility.
  • The fourth chamber is designed to house return pumps and heaters.
  • The R80 and R80CS include a built-in ATO reservoir positioned in front of the first chamber for convenient top-off management.

Product Specifications

Tank Specifications

  • Dimensions: 31.5 x 31.5 x 14.17" / 80 x 80 x 36CM
  • Glass Thickness: 12mm
  • Volume: 52 gallons / 192L
  • Total Volume (Including Sump): 76 gallons / 277L

Stand Specifications

  • Dimensions: 31.5 x 31.5 x 35.43" / 80 x 80 x 90CM
  • Door Count: 4
  • Storage Drawers: 1

Sump Specifications

  • Dimensions: 21.3 x 28.74 x 17.72" / 54.1 x 73 x 45CM
  • Volume: 24 gallons / 85L
  • Filter Bags: 3
  • Total Chambers: 4

Accessory Clearance

  • Chamber 1: 3.94 x 13.58" / 10 x 34.5CM
  • Chamber 2: 9.45 x 16.73" / 24 x 42.5CM
  • Chamber 3: 6.3 x 8.66" / 16 x 22CM
  • Chamber 4: 6.69 x 7.48" / 17 x 19CM

Available in multiple tank sizes and two stand colors

  • UNS R60
  • UNS R75
  • UNS R80CS

products.title: UNS Touch Up Pen - Aluminum Stand
products.body_html:
Keep your UNS Aluminum Stand looking fresh and well-maintained with a touch-up paint pen. Designed for quick, easy fixes, it’s ideal for covering small chips, scratches, or signs of wear that can occur during the aquascape setup process, tank transportation, or regular aquarium maintenance.

The precision tip allows for controlled application and smooth, even coverage, making it easy to restore the original white or grey finish of your aquarium stand without streaks or mess. Whether you’re doing a quick touch-up at home or prepping a display for an event, the UNS Touch Up Pen offers a simple and effective way to keep your tank stand looking sharp over time.

Key Features and Important Notes

products.title: Bucephalandra Brownie Kapuas
products.body_html:
Common Name: Bucephalandra Kapuas

Bucephalandra Kapuas is a compact epiphyte featuring dark, moody foliage and a subtle metallic sheen. This Buce variant displays narrow, ruffled oval-shaped leaves that range from deep green to brownish hues, often developing richer coloration under stronger lighting. Ideal for nano tanks, shrimp setups, and detailed hardscape work, Bucephalandra Kapuas adds texture and depth without overwhelming a layout. A reliable choice for aquascapers looking for hardy structure and understated color contrast.

Like other epiphytic aquatic plants, Kapuas grows from a rhizome and should be attached to rock or driftwood. It thrives in low to medium light and does well in both low-tech aquascapes and CO2-injected aquariums. While growth is slow, stable water parameters and regular fertilization will encourage fuller, healthier clumps over time.

Notes:

  • Bucephalandra is an epiphytic aquarium plant, and prefers to be attached to aquarium hardscape.
  • Do not cover the rhizome when planting the Bucephalandra in substrate. This will cause melt and rot.
  • Please be sure to remove this plant from its pot. Remove the rockwool surrounding the roots and attach it to the hardscape. For instructions on how to properly prep "potted" aquarium plants, click here.
  • Bucephalandra’s final size, coloration, and growth rate are dependent upon the condition of the aquarium.
  • Photos are a representation of what you will receive and may vary.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters can result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • CO2 injection will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Family Name: Araceae
Endemic To: Borneo
Leaves: Small to medium (1-2 inches)
pH: 5.5-7.5
Care: Easy
Light: Low to Medium
Co2: Not necessary, but recommended
Propagation: Cut by rhizome
Growth rate: Slow

products.title: Bucephalandra Artemis
products.body_html:
Common Name: Bucephalandra Artemis

Bucephalandra ‘Artemis’ is a striking and relatively rare aquatic plant, prized in the aquascaping hobby for its unique dark green tones with subtle iridescent highlights. Its palm tree-like leaves are small, elongated, and slightly wavy, often showing a silvery sheen when grown in moderate to high light, giving the foreground or midground a touch of “glow” in the planted tank.

Like most Buce, it grows slowly and prefers being attached to hardscape like driftwood or rocks rather than being planted directly in substrate. It benefits from stable water parameters, and while Bucephalandra doesn’t demand CO2 injection and can thrive in low-tech aquariums, adding it can encourage faster growth and more vibrant coloration.

Notes:

  • Bucephalandra is an epiphytic aquarium plant, and prefers to be attached to aquarium hardscape.
  • Do not cover the rhizome when planting the Bucephalandra in substrate. This will cause melt and rot.
  • Please be sure to remove this plant from its pot. Remove the rockwool surrounding the roots and attach it to the hardscape. For instructions on how to properly prep "potted" aquarium plants, click here.

  • Photos are a representation of what you will receive and may vary.

products.title: Anchor Moss on Stainless Steel
products.body_html:
Common Name: Anchor Moss, Vesicularia

Anchor Moss is a very densely ramified, bushy moss with a soft, delicate texture, similar to needles of a pine tree. Its dense stems are broad and shoot out at nearly right angles. Planting moss in the aquarium foreground or midground provides compact softness, especially in smaller tanks. As one of the lighter-colored green aquarium mosses, it tends to brightly stand out and add contrast to any brown or grey surfaces.

This aquatic moss can grow in both emersed and submerged conditions, and attaches well to substrate, driftwood, or rocks. While it's known to grow slowly, Anchor Moss has a wide range of light tolerance. The greater the light, the darker the moss will grow.

Notes:

  • Aquarium moss is extremely hardy and will survive in a wide range of aquarium parameters.
  • Please note that most moss species can contain brown bits, and while suitable for low tech tanks, CO2 injection will yield better growth and coloration.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Genus: Vesicularia

Family Name: Hypnaceae

Origin: Singapore

Height: 3.9” – 7.9”

pH: 5 - 7

Care: Medium

Light: Low to High

Co2: Medium

Propagation: Dividing off daughter plants.

Growth Rate: Slow

products.title: Ludwigia Sphaerocarpa
products.body_html:
Ludwigia Sphaerocarpa is an interesting species of Ludwigia that is native to the United States and commonly found in moist, wet coastal areas along Massachusetts down to eastern Texas. Unlike other species in the Ludwigia genus, Ludwigia Sphaerocarpa is not characterized by quick-growing stems. Instead, Ludwigia Sphaerocarpa is a suitable choice for the foreground and midground area of planted aquariums. With consistent pruning, this aquarium plant displays attractive crowns that grow low and range in red to orange colors. Unlike the usual vertical growth, this particular species grows low and exhibits horizontal growth patterns. To maintain this aquatic plant, relatively strong aquarium lighting should be used and this plant should not be placed in areas that receive shade. 

Doing so results in a dull green color along with slower growth. Like other stem plants, propagation is simple. Cut the stem and replant into quality aquarium soil.  

Notes:

  • Ludwigia can be very fragile. Be sure to handle this aquarium plant with care.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • Remove the cotton surrounding the roots and plant into a quality substrate. For instructions on how to properly prep aquarium plants, click here.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Genus: Ludwigia

Family Name: Onagraceae

Origin: Southeastern U.S.

Height: 4-12”

pH: 4.5 - 7.0

Care: Easy

Light: Medium to High

Co2: Recommended

Propagation: Cuttings

Growth Rate: Fast

products.title: Cryptocoryne Striolata
products.body_html:
Common Name: Cryptocoryne Striolata 

Cryptocoryne Striolata is an easy plant, perfect for beginners. This rare Crypt can be found from the Sarawak region of Borneo growing on the rocks along the riverbed or along submerged tree roots. It grows at a slow rate compared to other Crypts and can be grown both submerged and emersed. To propagate, simply cut off new plantlets growing from the mother rhizome

products.title: Aquario Neo Media - Pure - Quad
products.body_html:
Aquario NEO Media - Pure - Quad

Aquario NEO Media is a high-quality filter media that is made from natural materials in Korea. With various pore sizes, it provides space for different strains of bacteria to live, ensuring that filtration cycles are stable. This filter media is manufactured at high temperatures to retain its strength and goes through a 5 step process to ensure quality. This filter media is available in 3 different forms to suit all types of aquariums. 

Aquario NEO MEDIA Pure is neutral in nature. This filter media is perfect for planted aquariums, shrimp tanks, and any other type of aquarium.

Use and Precautions:

  1. Be sure to rinse your media under clean water a few times.
  2. The effectiveness of NEO Media can be affected by the water used and objects within the aquarium.
  3. Routine maintenance should be performed for aquarium filter media.
  4. Shapes and sizes can vary due to use of natural materials.
  5. Keep out of the reach of children and pets.

products.title: Aquario Neo Media - Soft - Quad
products.body_html:

Aquario NEO Media is a high-quality filter media that is made from natural materials in Korea. This filter media is manufactured at high temperatures to retain its strength and goes through a 5 step process to ensure quality. products.title: UNS Pre Filter Pad
products.body_html:
Compatible with UNS Reef Systems ONLY.

The UNS Pre Filter Pad is the first line of defense for your aquarium filter. It’s a simple but effective mechanical filter media for keeping the filter socks in your reef sump running cleaner & longer. Designed with a multi-layer honeycomb structure, it captures detritus and fine debris before it reaches your filter socks, helping extend their lifespan and improve overall efficiency.

Made to integrate seamlessly with the Ultum Nature Systems Reef System, this Pre Filter Pad is easy to install, remove, and rinse, making your routine aquarium maintenance quicker and less messy. A small upgrade that goes a long way in maintaining stable, clear aquarium water.

Key Features and Important Notes

  • Keeps detritus from breaking down into nitrates and phosphates.
  • Prevents excess waste from clogging finer media.

products.title: Blood Vomit (Centrolepis Drummondiana)
products.body_html:
Common Name: Blood Vomit

Centrolepis Drummondiana is a rare foreground aquarium plant with fine, grassy blades and subtle reddish tones at the base under strong light. It stays compact and low, making it a great choice for detailed planted tank layouts where texture matters. Best suited for high-tech aquariums, this plant rewards stable conditions with a unique look you won’t see in most fish tanks.

This species prefers bright lighting & consistent CO2 to maintain tight growth and healthy, vibrant color. Growth is on the slower side, so patience pays off, especially in well-maintained aquascape layouts where it won’t be shaded by taller stem plants.

Key Features & Important Notes:

  • Final coloration is dependent upon the conditions in which this plant is kept. This is a live plant, meaning size, shape, and color may vary.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Stable parameters are best when possible.

products.title: Eriocaulon Feather Duster
products.body_html:
Add delicate texture and movement to your aquascape with Eriocaulon ‘Feather Duster.’ This aquatic plant forms soft, feather-like blades that create a natural, elegant midground or background display. Its fine leaves respond beautifully to bright light and nutrients, giving your tank a lush, detailed look.

Eriocaulon ‘Feather Duster’ is a grassy aquarium plant ideal for aquascapers who enjoy a patient, rewarding planting experience. It thrives when paired with CO2 and regular fertilization, forming compact clumps that enhance both contrast and depth in your layout. With proper care, it brings a unique softness to any freshwater aquascape.

Key Features & Important Notes:

  • Minimal trimming is required, but removing old or dying leaves helps new growth.
  • Roots are delicate; avoid burying the crown too deeply in substrate.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Stable parameters are best when possible.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Genus: Eriocaulon
Family Name: Eriocaulaceae
Care: Moderate to advanced
Lighting: Medium to high
CO2: Recommended
pH: 6.0-7.0

products.title: Tree Moss
products.body_html:
Climacium dendroides, often called “Tree Moss,” is a unique moss with upright, branching growth that resembles tiny forest trees. Its dense, textured appearance adds a vertical element to terrariums or vivariums, creating natural-looking miniature landscapes.

This moss thrives in moist, shaded environments with stable humidity and can be attached to rocks, wood, or soil. This hardy terrestrial plant is ideal for new or experienced aquascapers looking to add intricate detail and depth to foregrounds or midgrounds while maintaining a low maintenance, long-lasting accent.

Key Features & Important Notes:

  • Occasional trimming will help maintain its shape and encourage denser growth.
  • This is not an aquarium plant. Submerging it completely will result in the death of this plant.
  • Photos are a representation of what you will receive. Size, shape and color may vary as this is a live plant.
  • Additional research should be done to provide optimum growing conditions for the plant to thrive.

products.title: Hygrophila Salicifolia Narrow Leaf
products.body_html:
Hygrophila salicifolia is an aquarium plant that is great for all skill levels. Often compared to Water Wisteria, this fast-growing Hygro is just as hardy while offering a more upright growth habit, making it an excellent choice for filling out the background of a planted aquarium. Like many aquatic plants, it may experience some initial melt as it adjusts to a new tank. Give it time to establish healthy roots and it will quickly rebound. Once settled, Hygrophila salicifolia grows vigorously and adapts well to a wide range of water conditions.

As long as aquarium parameters remain reasonably stable, this plant will continue producing new growth with minimal effort. In lower light setups, expect slightly leggier growth, while higher light and CO2 injection will produce thicker stems and faster development. If you're looking for a hardy, fast-growing stem plant, this is a great one to try.

Key Features & Important Notes: 

  • Photos are a representation of what you will receive. Size, shape and color may vary and should be expected with live plants.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Family: Acanthaceae
Region: Southeast Asia
Height: 6-12"
Care: Easy
Light: Low to Moderate
CO2: Not required, but recommended
Propagation: Trim stems and replant
Growth Rate: Fast

products.title: Marcgravia Sintenisii
products.body_html:
Common Name: Rainbow Marcgravia

Marcgravia sintenisii is a stunning tropical vine from the rainforests of South America. Its shingling leaves showcase a captivating gradient, shifting from red to orange to green, adding a vibrant splash of color to any terrarium or paludarium. The vines naturally climb toward the light, creating graceful, vertical displays.

This terrestrial plant thrives in low to medium light and prefers its roots and growing surfaces to stay slightly moist, while avoiding prolonged water on the leaves. With the right care, Marcgravia sintenisii rewards plant enthusiasts with striking coloration and elegant, cascading growth.

Key Features & Important Notes:

  • Shingling plants do best in environments with vertical surfaces to climb.
  • This is not an aquarium plant. Submerging it completely will result in the death of this plant.
  • Photos are a representation of what you will receive. Size, shape and color may vary as this is a live plant.
  • Additional research on Marcgravia sintenisii care should be done to provide optimum growing conditions for the plant to thrive.

products.title: Umbrella Moss
products.body_html:
Common Name: Giant Rose Moss

Rhodobryum giganteum is a unique moss that forms miniature rosettes resembling tiny, delicate roses. Native to the humid forests of Asia, this slow-growing moss thrives in high-humidity environments and adds a lush, ornamental touch to terrariums & paludariums. Each rosette gradually expands into dense clusters, creating a soft, textured carpet that accentuates rocks, driftwood, or substrate surfaces.

Ideal for hobbyists seeking a unique, low-maintenance moss, Umbrella Moss prefers moderate indirect light & consistent moisture. With proper care, it becomes a standout feature in any humid display, offering both elegance and a natural, forest-like aesthetic.

Key Features & Important Notes:

  • Each rosette typically grows about 0.5–1.5 cm in diameter. Clusters can slowly expand over time.
  • Attach to driftwood or rock to prevent rot.
  • Photos are a representation of what you will receive. Size, shape and color may vary as this is a live plant.
  • This is not an aquarium plant. Submerging it completely will result in the death of this plant.

products.title: Pellionia Repens
products.body_html:
Common Name: Trailing Watermelon Begonia, Watermelon Vine, Elatostema repens, Pellionia repens, Rainbow vine

Pellionia Repens, commonly named Trailing Watermelon Begonia, is an evergreen perennial that is great for at providing ground cover. Although called a Begonia, Pellionia Repens is not related to Begonias and is part of the Urticaceae family. The ovate leaves of the Watermelon Vine are dark green with randomly scattered patterns of light green to silver. Under more light the stems will become a pinkish red color and the leaves will develop a maroon tint.

Pellionia Repens thrive in high humidity environments and should be in soil that are constantly moist. Leaves may begin to brown if the humidity is low. Originating from tropical forest floors and understories, Pellionia Repens does not tolerate cold or high light well. Having a vine like growth pattern, Pellionia Repens can be planted in terrestrial setups, such as terrariums and paladariums to provide great coverage.

Key Features & Important Notes: 

  • Pellionia Repens is a terrestrial plant and being submerged will result in death of the plant
  • Pellionia Repens prefers being grown with high humidity, partial shade, and moist soil.
  • Watermelon Begonia is not actually a Begonia.
  • Plants may grow differently depending on the environment in which they are grown in.
  • Additional research should be done to provide optimum growing conditions for the plant to thrive.

Family Name: Urticaceae
Origin: Southeast Asia
Temperature: 40-80 F
Care: Easy
Light: Shaded Light 
Water: Medium
Propagation: Herbaceous stem cutting 
Growth rate: Medium

products.title: Mini Taiwan Moss on Stainless Steel
products.body_html:
Common Name: Mini Taiwan Moss (Taxiphyllum Alterans)

Taxiphyllum Sp. Taiwan Moss is a less common moss that is highly sought after by both expert and beginner hobbyists. Taiwan Moss has an appearance that is similar to Java Moss and Willow Moss making it grow in popularity quickly. Like all moss species, Taiwan Moss is extremely easy to care for and will adapt to a wide range of aquarium parameters and water conditions. Taiwan Moss has the capability to live in cold water tanks along with tropical aquariums. It doesn't require too much lighting and is a great hiding spot for small freshwater shrimp species or fish fry. It can be a slow grower, but if given the chance, is a beautiful addition a planted aquarium. It can be attached to hardscape material using glue, thread or ties and will eventually attach itself with time providing a beautiful aged look. 

Notes:

  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • For instructions on how to properly prep tissue culture aquarium plants, click here.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.

Family Name: Hypnaceae
Origin: Taiwan
pH: 6.5-7.5
Care: Easy
Light: Low
Co2: Not necessary
Growth: Moderate

products.title: Ranalisma Rostratum
products.body_html:
Common Name: Ranalisma Rostratum

Ranalisma Rostratum originates from the tropics in Asia. This aquatic plant is still very rare in the trade, difficult to get and is often spelled incorrectly making it more elusive to eager planted tank enthusiasts. This aquatic plant is peculiar in that when it is grown submerged, it appears completely different and its leaves will transform into very narrow blades that resemble a grass-like plant. It features a bright green color and is comparable to the popular aquatic plant, Helanthium Parvulum. When emersed, Ranalisma Rostratum will have much rounder leaves and can sprout small flowers that bear small spiky fruits. In order to grow this plant correctly, high lighting, CO2 injection and a nutrient dense substrate are some of the basic requirements for success. If these factors are provided, this aquatic plant will grow a nice dense lawn of green that will be a beautiful addition to a planted aquarium.

Family Name: Alismataceae

Origin: Asia

Height: 2-5"

pH: 6-7.5

Care: Medium

Light: Medium

Co2: Not Required

Propagation: Replant runners

Growth rate: Slow to Moderate

products.title: Echinodorus Palaefolius
products.body_html:
Common Name: Echinodorus Palaefolius

Echinodorus Palaefolius is naturally found in marshy type conditions. It features rounded leaves, but when submerged, this aquatic plant sports leaves that are a bit more narrow and lengthy. Like most swords, this variant is also a heavy root feeder and should be planted in a nutrient-rich aquarium substrate to provide optimal conditions. If planted in an inert substrate such as sand or gravel, care should be taken to provide another form of nutritious supplement whether via root tabs or a quality plant fertilizer. Newer leaves may produce a slight reddish tint and will fully color up as the plant matures. It has quite a few trade names and is often mistaken as some of the following: Latifolius, Echinodorus Rigidifolius, Echinodorus Subalatus, and Echinodorus Cordifolius.

With a nutrient rich substrate these plants will tolerate most lighting conditions, but moderate to high lighting is ideal. CO2 injection is not necessary but will help the plant grow faster if desired. Also a fast grower, regular pruning will be required to allow light filtration to lower levels of the plant. Echinodorus Palaefolius will grow submersed or emersed. The emersed form has the potential to produce lovely white flowers.

Notes:

  • Echinodorus is a very hardy species of aquatic plants. Most Echinodorus require nutrient-dense soil to thrive as they are heavy root feeders.
  • Echinodorus grow fairly large and are more suitable for larger planted aquariums.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • Please be sure to remove this plant from its pot. Remove the cotton surrounding the roots and plant into a quality substrate. For instructions on how to properly prep "potted" aquarium plants, click here.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Family Name: Alismataceae

Origin: South America

Height: 4-20”

pH: 6.5-7.5

Care: Easy

Light: Moderate

Co2: Not necessary

Propagation: Adventitious shoots

Growth rate: Fast

products.body_html:

Ozelot Red plants are very common in the aquarium hobby, for being a beautiful, colorful and easy plant to care for. They offer excellent and attractive coverage to the background of an aquascape. These plants are characterized by their narrowly elliptic submerged leaves that are brownish-red. During their sprouting phase, they develop dark red spots.

With a nutrient-rich substrate, these plants will tolerate most lighting conditions, but moderate to high lighting is ideal. CO2 injection is not necessary but will help the plant grow faster if desired. Ozelot Red plants will grow submersed or emersed. 

Notes:

  • Echinodorus is a very hardy species of aquatic plants. Most Echinodorus require nutrient-dense soil to thrive as they are heavy root feeders.
  • Echinodorus grow fairly large and are more suitable for larger planted aquariums.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • Please be sure to remove this plant from its pot. Remove the cotton surrounding the roots and plant into a quality substrate. For instructions on how to properly prep "potted" aquarium plants, click here.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Origin: South America

pH: 5-7

products.title: Echinodorus Impai
products.body_html:
Amazon sword plants are very common in the aquarium hobby, being that they are beautiful and easy to care for. They offer excellent and attractive coverage to the background of an aquascape. These plants have short stems that grow in a rosette, bearing long and ovoid leaves with a pointed tip. Their leaves are dark or bright green in color with prominent veins and slightly wavy shape.

Amazon sword plants can get pretty huge, making them a great fit for a large tank. Their large leaves can easily restrict light to other plants if they are not pruned regularly. These plants propagate through side shoots off of their leaves, which can be trimmed and replanted into the substrate. To control the size of your Amazon sword plant you can remove the side shoots and older leaves.

With a nutrient rich substrate these plants will tolerate most lighting conditions, but moderate to high lighting is ideal. CO2 injection is not necessary but will help the plant grow faster if desired. Amazon sword plants will grow submersed or emersed. Emersed plants produce lovely white flowers.

Notes:

  • Echinodorus is a very hardy species of aquatic plants. Most Echinodorus require nutrient-dense soil to thrive as they are heavy root feeders.
  • Echinodorus grow fairly large and are more suitable for larger planted aquariums.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • Please be sure to remove this plant from its pot. Remove the cotton surrounding the roots and plant into a quality substrate. For instructions on how to properly prep "potted" aquarium plants, click here.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.

products.title: Echinodorus Jaguar
products.body_html:
Common Name: Echinodorus Jaguar

Echinodorus species are staples in the aquarium hobby. Echinodorus Jaguar offers excellent and attractive coverage to the background of an aquascape. These plants have short stems that grow in a rosette, bearing long and ovoid leaves with a rounded tip. Their leaves are bright green in color with prominent veins. Amazon sword plants, in general, can get pretty huge, making them a great fit for a large tank.  Their large leaves can easily restrict light to other plants if they are not pruned regularly. These plants propagate through side shoots off of their leaves, which can be trimmed and replanted into the substrate. To control the size of your Amazon sword plant you can remove the side shoots and older leaves.

With a nutrient rich substrate these aquatic plants will tolerate most lighting conditions, but moderate to high lighting is ideal. CO2 injection is not necessary but will help the plant grow faster if desired. Amazon sword plants will grow submersed or emersed. Emersed plants produce lovely white flowers.

Notes:

  • Echinodorus is a very hardy species of aquatic plants. Most Echinodorus require nutrient-dense soil to thrive as they are heavy root feeders.
  • Echinodorus grow fairly large and are more suitable for larger planted aquariums.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • Please be sure to remove this plant from its pot. Remove the cotton surrounding the roots and plant into a quality substrate. For instructions on how to properly prep "potted" aquarium plants, click here.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Common Name: Echinodorus Jaguar

Family Name: Alismaaceae

Height: 8-16"

pH: 5.5-8

Care: Easy

Light: Low-Moderate

Co2: Not Required

Propagation: Separate new plantlets from the root

Growth rate: Moderate

products.title: UNS Livestock Holding System
products.body_html:
The UNS Livestock Holding System offers a clean, efficient solution for housing, showcasing, quarantining, and breeding aquatic livestock. Constructed with ultra-clear glass, precision aluminum framing, and a modular multi-tank design, it provides independent control of each compartment while sharing a centralized filtration system.

The system features three tanks, each outfitted with three adjustable baffles that allow you to customize individual holding areas, for a total of 16 sections. Thoughtful plumbing details include individually adjustable flow, an integrated drain line, and a smoked glass sump, simplifying maintenance while keeping livestock healthy and visually front-and-center.

Key Features

  • Central Plumbing — Each tank features its own ball-valve drain, making water changes quick, controlled, and mess-free
  • Refill Line — Quick and easy way to top off fish bags for trades, events, quarantine transfers, or tank moves without disrupting the rest of the system
  • Individualized flow for precise control over water flow to suit the needs of every fish or compartment
  • Force drain to pull water straight from the bottom of the tank, removing settled waste and debris

Product Specifications

  • Display Tank Dimensions: 35.4 x 11.81 x 9.84" / 90 x 30 x 25CM

The UNS Betta Holding System lets you safely house multiple bettas in one organized, efficient setup. It features 32 partitioned compartments, adjustable flow control, and centralized filtration to keep water clean and fish healthy, while ultra-clear glass and sleek aluminum framing make every fish easy to see.

It’s ideal for breeding projects, temporary quarantine, or showcasing prized bettas, offering durability and convenience in a compact footprint that simplifies maintenance and water changes.

The Chihiros Magnetic Stirrer is designed to keep liquid fertilizers thoroughly mixed for consistent and accurate dosing. The four independent stirring heads ensure supplements stay evenly blended, preventing settling and maintaining uniform nutrient distribution over time.

Built for reliability and everyday use, the stirrer operates quietly within a wide temperature range and is compatible with standard household power. It pairs seamlessly with Chihiros dosing systems, making it an ideal addition to any automated fertilization setup.

products.title: Aponogeton Natans
products.body_html:
Common name: Aponogeton Natans

Aponogteon Natans is an aquatic bulb plant with long green wavy leaves that originally comes from stagnant waters in India and Sri Lanka. Like most Aponogeton species, this particular aquatic plant can grow quite large, making it a great addition as a background plant focal plant. With optimal care, Aponogeton Natans flowers frequently and is a great additional to planted aquariums.

Half of the bulb should be buried into the substrate to facilitate growth. Avoid completely burying the bulb as this can promote bulb rot. 

Notes:

  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • Aponogeton grows fairly large making this aquarium plant suitable for larger planted aquariums. 
  • Please be sure to remove this plant from its lead bunch. Remove the cotton surrounding the roots and plant into a quality substrate. For instructions on how to properly prep "bunched" aquarium plants, click here.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Origin: Sri Lanka

Height: 8-20”

pH: 6.5-7.5

Light: Moderate to High

Co2: Not necessary

Growth Rate: Medium

products.title: Dymax eZ Filter
products.body_html:
The Dymax eZ Filter is an efficient and well performing internal filter that will help maintain proper aquarium health. Filter media within the filter have a high surface area, making it perfect for beneficial bacteria to colonize and thrive. The eZ Filter has all its media separated with a filter basket and includes white wool (mechanical filtration), activated carbon (chemical filtration), and biological sponge (biological filter). Filter media should be cleaned or replaced regularly to maintain optimum performance.

The Dymax eZ Filter is powered by a powerful Dymax pump. It will draw water from both the top and bottom levels of the water column, and water will be expelled towards the middle. This internal filter is suitable for both freshwater and marine aquariums. The Dymax IQ Skimmer can be attached onto the Dymax eZ Filter for marine aquariums so that protein skimming and filtering can all be done by one unit. This internal filter is suitable for tank sizes 60-90cm in length and 30cm in height.

Aquarium water should be used when cleaning filter media to ensure that the beneficial bacteria is not killed.

products.title: Spathiphyllum Wallisii - Peace Lily
products.body_html:
Spathiphyllum Wallisii, more commonly known as a Peace Lily is a marsh plant originating from Central America. Peace Lilies are not true aquatic plants and it is not recommended to permanently submerge the plant. Spathiphyllum Wallisii will do best when grown in a terrarium or paludarium setup where its roots can stay moist. It requires medium to high lighting and is a relatively slow grower. If taken care of well, Peace Lilies can bloom twice a year and their flowers are a beautiful sight to see. Propagation is also simple and can be done by separating the rhizome and replanting. Although this plant is not a true lily and is not poisonous, care should be taken when handling as it can cause some discomfort if ingested. Keep this plant out of the reach of children and pets as well!  

Family Name:  Araceae

Origin: Central America

Height: 5-12”

pH: 5.5-7.5

Care: Easy

Light: Moderate to High

Co2: Not Required

Propagation: Cut lateral runners and replant

Growth rate: Slow

products.body_html:
The UNS Stainless Steel Gravel Rake is an essential maintenance tool for planted aquariums and aquascapes. Designed to level chunkier substrate and loosen surface debris, it helps keep your substrate level and clean. Constructed for durability, this gravel rake is also rust-resistant, making it suitable for long-term use in freshwater aquariums. A dependable tool for aquascapers who value clean lines, healthy substrate, and thoughtful tank maintenance.

Key Features:

  • Stainless steel construction
  • Rust-resistant for long-lasting performance
  • Ideal for leveling substrate and routine maintenance

Care & Safety:

  • Store safely away from children and pets

products.title: Bucephalandra Sanggau UNS Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
Common Name: Buce Sanggau

Bucephalandra Sanggau is a variety of Buce that features variable color and slightly ruffled leaves. This type of aquatic plant is great for adding extra texture and color to midground areas of an aquascape. Like other Buce, it is relatively hardy and will adapt to a range of water conditions. As a baseline, we recommend adding Buce to established tanks for better transition and less melt. Cultivated through tissue culture, Bucephalandra Sanggau is free from pests and contaminants, ensuring a healthy start in your aquarium. Its low maintenance requirements make it suitable for both beginners and seasoned aquarists. Whether attached to driftwood, rocks, or planted in the substrate, this plant adds natural beauty to any freshwater setup.

Notes:

  • Bucephalandra is an epiphytic aquarium plant, so it can be attached to aquarium hardscape.
  • Do not plant Bucephalandra in the substrate and cover the rhizome. This will cause it to melt and rot.
  • Bucephalandra’s final size, coloration and growth rate are dependent upon the condition of the aquarium Photos are a representation of what you will receive and may vary.
  • Tissue culture plants are grown in vitro from a state of the art laboratory specialized in propagating aquatic plants. They provide the best option for those who want to ensure that absolutely zero unwanted pests are introduced into the aquarium.
  • Remove tissue culture gel before planting.  Simply wash the gel away or soak the plant in water and squeeze the gel off with your fingers.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • For instructions on how to properly plant tissue culture aquarium plants, click here.
  • CO2 injection will yield better growth.

Family Name: Araceae

Endemic To: Borneo

pH: 6-7.5

Care: Easy

Light: Low to Medium

Co2: Not necessary but recommended 

Propagation: Cut by rhizome

Growth rate: Slow

products.title: Christmas Moss Tree
products.body_html:
Christmas Moss Tree

Celebrate the season by adding a festive touch to your aquarium with one or two live moss trees. Featuring lush Christmas Moss carefully pre-wrapped, this piece is a fun seasonal accent for your planted tank or terrarium.

Christmas Moss is a low-light, low-maintenance choice that’s easy for hobbyists of all experience levels to keep. With proper care and patience, it will continue to grow and fill out naturally. Simply place the tree into your aquascape. As the moss grows, new trimmings can be cut and reattached to rocks or driftwood.

Notes:

  • Aquarium moss is extremely hardy and will survive in a wide range of aquarium parameters.
  • Please note, most moss species can contain brown bits and while suitable for low tech tanks, CO2 injection will yield better growth and coloration.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Care: Easy
Light: Low
Co2: Not necessary
Propagation: Separate
Growth rate: Moderate

products.title: Twinstar Sterilizer for Fish
products.body_html:
Twinstar Yotta is a powerful sterilizer that aid in the prevention of common fish diseases. These products work by eliminating pathogens found in the water column along with mold spores that may find their way into the aquarium. The Twinstar Yotta is designed to have no impact on biological balances within the aquarium tank and are safe to use with tropical fish.

Fish disease is commonly caused by a mix of 3 factors. These factors include the environment, basic fish immunity, and pathogens. If the environment is compromised and immunity is low, the presence of pathogens and risk increases. Twinstar sterilizer models for fish effectively eliminate fish disease through strong sterilizing factors, so it doesn’t need to run 24 hours like UV sterilizer. This device will not harm aerobic bacteria and helps to kill pathogens, so it’s safe. On top of this, Twinstar's lifespan is triple that of general UV sterilizers! 

Please note, this device will not cure existing ailments and is intended to be used for prevention. It is recommended to use in combination with good husbandry for best results.
